# vue-metamorph

> Codemod framework for Vue projects. Transform source code reliably at scale using ASTs instead of regex.

vue-metamorph parses Vue SFC, JavaScript, TypeScript, CSS, SCSS, SASS, LESS, and Stylus files into ASTs. You write plugins that traverse and mutate these ASTs, and vue-metamorph detects your changes and applies them back to the source code.

## Installation

```bash
# Scaffold a new project
npx vue-metamorph my-codemod-name

# Or install in an existing project
npm install -D vue-metamorph
```

## Plugin Types

### CodemodPlugin

Mutates ASTs to transform code. Returns the number of transforms applied (used for stats).

```ts
import type { CodemodPlugin } from 'vue-metamorph';

const myCodemod: CodemodPlugin = {
  type: 'codemod',
  name: 'my-transform',
  transform({ scriptASTs, sfcAST, styleASTs, filename, utils }) {
    let count = 0;
    // Mutate the ASTs here. vue-metamorph detects changes automatically.
    return count;
  }
};
```

### ManualMigrationPlugin

Finds nodes that need human attention. Do NOT mutate the AST in manual migrations.

```ts
import type { ManualMigrationPlugin } from 'vue-metamorph';

const myManualMigration: ManualMigrationPlugin = {
  type: 'manual',
  name: 'find-deprecated-api',
  find({ scriptASTs, sfcAST, styleASTs, filename, report, utils }) {
    // Call report(node, message) for each node needing attention
  }
};
```

### Plugin Context

Both plugin types receive a context object with:

- `scriptASTs: VueProgram[]` - For .vue files, one Program AST per `<script>` block. For JS/TS files, a single-element array.
- `sfcAST: VDocumentFragment | null` - The Vue template AST. Null for non-.vue files.
- `styleASTs: postcss.Root[]` - PostCSS ASTs for `<style>` blocks or standalone CSS files.
- `filename: string` - Absolute path of the file being processed.
- `utils` - Object containing `builders`, `astHelpers`, `traverseScriptAST`, and `traverseTemplateAST`.
- `opts: Record<string, any>` - CLI options passed through.

`VueProgram` extends ESTree's `Program` with an `isScriptSetup: boolean` property indicating whether it represents a `<script setup>` block.

## Core API

### transform(code, filename, plugins, opts?)

Parses source code, runs codemod plugins against it, and returns transformed code.

```ts
import { transform } from 'vue-metamorph';

const result = transform(sourceCode, 'file.vue', [myCodemod]);
result.code;  // transformed source code
result.stats; // array of [pluginName, transformCount] tuples
```

The filename determines how code is parsed:
- `.vue` - Parsed as Vue SFC (template + scripts + styles)
- `.js`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, `.tsx` - Parsed as JavaScript/TypeScript
- `.css`, `.scss`, `.sass`, `.less`, `.styl` - Parsed as CSS

### findManualMigrations(code, filename, plugins, opts?)

Runs manual migration plugins and returns reports.

```ts
import { findManualMigrations } from 'vue-metamorph';

const reports = findManualMigrations(sourceCode, 'file.vue', [myManualMigration]);
// Each report has: message, file, snippet, pluginName, lineStart, lineEnd, columnStart, columnEnd
```

### createVueMetamorphCli(options)

Creates a CLI runner with file globbing and progress reporting.

```ts
import { createVueMetamorphCli } from 'vue-metamorph';

const { run, abort } = createVueMetamorphCli({
  plugins: [myCodemod, myManualMigration],
  silent: false,
  onProgress({ totalFiles, filesProcessed, stats, done, errors, manualMigrations }) {
    // called after each file is processed
  },
});

run(); // parses process.argv for --files and --plugins options
```

CLI options: `--files <glob>` (default: `'**/src/**/*'`), `--plugins <glob...>` (default: `'*'`), `--list-plugins`.

## AST Helpers

Available via `utils.astHelpers` in plugin context, or importable directly:

```ts
import { astHelpers } from 'vue-metamorph';
```

### findFirst(ast, matcher)

Finds the first node matching a partial object pattern. Uses deep partial matching (lodash `isMatch`). Works with both script ASTs (ESTree) and template ASTs (vue-eslint-parser).

```ts
// Find the first <div> in the template
astHelpers.findFirst(sfcAST, { type: 'VElement', name: 'div' });

// Find the first call to console.log in a script
astHelpers.findFirst(scriptAST, {
  type: 'CallExpression',
  callee: {
    type: 'MemberExpression',
    object: { type: 'Identifier', name: 'console' },
    property: { type: 'Identifier', name: 'log' },
  },
});
```

Returns the matching node, or `null`.

### findAll(ast, matcher)

Same as `findFirst` but returns an array of all matching nodes.

```ts
// Find all <MyComponent> elements
astHelpers.findAll(sfcAST, { type: 'VElement', name: 'MyComponent' });

// Find all v-if directives
astHelpers.findAll(sfcAST, {
  type: 'VAttribute',
  directive: true,
  key: { type: 'VDirectiveKey', name: { name: 'if' } },
});

// Find all @click handlers
astHelpers.findAll(sfcAST, {
  type: 'VAttribute',
  directive: true,
  key: {
    type: 'VDirectiveKey',
    name: { name: 'on' },
    argument: { type: 'VIdentifier', name: 'click' },
  },
});

// Find all static class attributes
astHelpers.findAll(sfcAST, {
  type: 'VAttribute',
  directive: false,
  key: { name: 'class' },
});
```

### findImportDeclaration(ast, moduleSpecifier)

Finds an existing import declaration for a module in a script AST.

```ts
const vueImport = astHelpers.findImportDeclaration(scriptAST, 'vue');
// Returns the ImportDeclaration node, or null
```

### createNamedImport(ast, moduleSpecifier, importName, localName?)

Adds a named import. Merges into existing import declarations for the same module. Skips duplicates.

```ts
// import { defineComponent } from 'vue';
astHelpers.createNamedImport(scriptAST, 'vue', 'defineComponent');

// import { map as lodashMap } from 'lodash-es';
astHelpers.createNamedImport(scriptAST, 'lodash-es', 'map', 'lodashMap');
```

### createDefaultImport(ast, moduleSpecifier, importName)

Adds a default import. Same merging/dedup logic as createNamedImport.

```ts
// import Vue from 'vue';
astHelpers.createDefaultImport(scriptAST, 'vue', 'Vue');
```

### createNamespaceImport(ast, moduleSpecifier, namespaceName)

Adds a namespace import. Same merging/dedup logic as createNamedImport.

```ts
// import * as _ from 'lodash-es';
astHelpers.createNamespaceImport(scriptAST, 'lodash-es', '_');
```

### findVueComponentOptions(ast, isSfc)

Finds Options API object expressions: `defineComponent()`, `Vue.extend()`, `Vue.component()`, `Vue.mixin()`, `new Vue()`, and (when isSfc=true) the default export.

```ts
for (const scriptAST of scriptASTs) {
  const options = astHelpers.findVueComponentOptions(scriptAST, sfcAST !== null);
  for (const obj of options) {
    // obj is an ObjectExpression node
  }
}
```

## AST Traversal

### traverseScriptAST(ast, visitors)

Alias for the ast-types `visit` function. Traverses ESTree/Babel AST nodes.

```ts
const { traverseScriptAST } = utils;

traverseScriptAST(scriptAST, {
  visitCallExpression(path) {
    // path.node is the CallExpression
    // Mutate path.node to make changes
    return this.traverse(path); // continue traversal
  }
});
```

Visitor methods are named `visit<NodeType>`. You must either call `this.traverse(path)` to continue into children, or `return false` to skip children.

### traverseTemplateAST(ast, visitors)

Alias for vue-eslint-parser's `traverseNodes`. Traverses Vue template AST nodes.

```ts
const { traverseTemplateAST } = utils;

traverseTemplateAST(sfcAST, {
  enterNode(node) {
    // called when entering each node
  },
  leaveNode(node) {
    // called when leaving each node
  },
});
```

## Template Builders

Available via `utils.builders` in plugin context. After inserting new nodes, call `builders.setParents(sfcAST)` to fix parent references.

### vElement(name, startTag, children, namespace?)

Creates an HTML element or Vue component node. End tag is created automatically.

```ts
// <div class="container">Hello</div>
builders.vElement(
  'div',
  builders.vStartTag([
    builders.vAttribute(builders.vIdentifier('class'), builders.vLiteral('container')),
  ], false),
  [builders.vText('Hello')],
);
```

### vStartTag(attributes, selfClosing, leadingComment?)

Creates a start tag with attributes and/or directives.

### vAttribute(key, value)

Creates a static attribute.

```ts
// class="active"
builders.vAttribute(builders.vIdentifier('class'), builders.vLiteral('active'));

// disabled (boolean attribute, no value)
builders.vAttribute(builders.vIdentifier('disabled'), null);
```

### vDirective(key, value)

Creates a Vue directive. Note: AST type is `'VAttribute'` with `directive: true`.

```ts
// v-if="visible"
builders.vDirective(
  builders.vDirectiveKey(builders.vIdentifier('if')),
  builders.vExpressionContainer(builders.identifier('visible')),
);

// :key="item.id"
builders.vDirective(
  builders.vDirectiveKey(
    builders.vIdentifier('bind', ':'),
    builders.vIdentifier('key'),
  ),
  builders.vExpressionContainer(
    builders.memberExpression(builders.identifier('item'), builders.identifier('id')),
  ),
);
```

### vDirectiveKey(name, argument?, modifiers?)

Creates the key part of a directive: `v-name:argument.modifier1.modifier2`.

### vExpressionContainer(expression, leadingComment?)

Creates an expression container (`{{ }}` or directive value).

### vIdentifier(name, rawName?)

Creates an identifier for attribute/directive names. `rawName` controls what gets printed.

```ts
builders.vIdentifier('bind', ':');  // name is 'bind', prints as ':'
builders.vIdentifier('on', '@');    // name is 'on', prints as '@'
builders.vIdentifier('slot', '#'); // name is 'slot', prints as '#'
```

### vLiteral(value)

Creates a string literal for static attribute values.

### vText(value, leadingComment?)

Creates a text node.

### vForExpression(left, right)

Creates a `v-for` expression: `left in right`.

### vOnExpression(body)

Creates a `v-on` expression with multiple statements.

### vFilterSequenceExpression(expression, filters) / vFilter(callee, args)

Creates Vue 2 filter syntax nodes.

### vDocumentFragment(children)

Creates a root document fragment node.

### htmlComment(value, leadingComment?)

Creates an HTML comment node.

### setParents(node)

Traverses a node tree and sets `parent` references on all descendants. Call after inserting new nodes.

## Script Builders

`utils.builders` also includes all ast-types builders for creating JavaScript/TypeScript AST nodes:

```ts
builders.literal('hello');           // string literal
builders.identifier('myVar');        // identifier
builders.callExpression(callee, args); // function call
builders.memberExpression(obj, prop);  // property access
builders.binaryExpression('+', left, right);
builders.importDeclaration(specifiers, source);
// ... and all other ESTree node types
```

See ast-types documentation and AST Explorer (with @babel/parser) for the full list.

## SFC Template AST Node Types

The template AST uses vue-eslint-parser node types:

- **VDocumentFragment** - Root node. `sfcAST` points to this. Children: VElement, VText, VExpressionContainer.
- **VElement** - HTML element or Vue component. Has `name`, `startTag`, `children`, `endTag`.
- **VStartTag** - Opening tag with `attributes` array (VAttribute or VDirective) and `selfClosing` boolean.
- **VAttribute** - Static attribute (`directive: false`). Has `key: VIdentifier` and `value: VLiteral | null`.
- **VDirective** - Vue directive (`directive: true`, type is still `'VAttribute'`). Has `key: VDirectiveKey` and `value: VExpressionContainer | null`.
- **VDirectiveKey** - Directive name/argument/modifiers. `name: VIdentifier`, `argument: VIdentifier | VExpressionContainer | null`, `modifiers: VIdentifier[]`.
- **VExpressionContainer** - Wraps JS expressions in directive values and `{{ }}` interpolations.
- **VIdentifier** - Attribute/directive names. `name` is normalized, `rawName` is as-written (e.g. `':'` for v-bind shorthand).
- **VLiteral** - Static attribute value string.
- **VText** - Plain text content.
- **VEndTag** - Closing tag.
- **HtmlComment** - HTML comment.

Directive shorthand mapping:

| Syntax | `key.name.name` | `key.name.rawName` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `v-if` | `'if'` | `'if'` |
| `v-for` | `'for'` | `'for'` |
| `v-model` | `'model'` | `'model'` |
| `:prop` | `'bind'` | `':'` |
| `@click` | `'on'` | `'@'` |
| `#default` | `'slot'` | `'#'` |

Special expression types inside VExpressionContainer:
- **VForExpression** - `left` (patterns) `in` `right` (collection)
- **VOnExpression** - Multiple statements in `body`
- **VSlotScopeExpression** - Slot scope `params`
- **VFilterSequenceExpression** / **VFilter** - Vue 2 filter syntax

## Testing Codemods

Use the `transform` function directly for test-driven development:

```ts
import { transform } from 'vue-metamorph';

it('should remove all v-ifs', () => {
  const input = `
<template>
  <div v-if="condition">Hello</div>
</template>
`;

  const expected = `
<template>
  <div>Hello</div>
</template>
`;

  expect(transform(input, 'file.vue', [myCodemod]).code).toBe(expected);
});
```

## Complete Example: Rename a Component

```ts
import { transform, type CodemodPlugin } from 'vue-metamorph';

const renameComponent: CodemodPlugin = {
  type: 'codemod',
  name: 'rename-old-button-to-new-button',
  transform({ scriptASTs, sfcAST, utils: { astHelpers, builders, traverseScriptAST } }) {
    let count = 0;

    // Rename in template
    if (sfcAST) {
      const elements = astHelpers.findAll(sfcAST, { type: 'VElement', name: 'OldButton' });
      for (const el of elements) {
        el.rawName = 'NewButton';
        el.name = 'NewButton';
        count++;
      }
    }

    // Rename in imports
    for (const scriptAST of scriptASTs) {
      traverseScriptAST(scriptAST, {
        visitImportDeclaration(path) {
          if (path.node.source.value === '@/components/OldButton.vue') {
            path.node.source.value = '@/components/NewButton.vue';
            count++;
          }
          return this.traverse(path);
        }
      });
    }

    return count;
  }
};
```

## AST Explorer

Use [AST Explorer](https://astexplorer.net) to visualize the AST for any code snippet.

| Source Type | Parser |
| --- | --- |
| Vue SFC `<template>` | `vue-eslint-parser` / `@babel/parser` |
| Vue SFC `<script>` | `@babel/parser` |
| Vue SFC `<style>` | `postcss` |
| JavaScript / TypeScript | `@babel/parser` |
| CSS / SCSS / LESS | `postcss` with appropriate parser |

## Code Formatting Note

vue-metamorph produces syntactically correct code but does not format it. Run a formatter (eslint, prettier, etc.) after transformation.
